During Wintersday , randomly dropped by PvE foes , except certain Wintersday quest foes such as during In Grenth's Defense and You're a Mean One, Mr. Grenth .
	During The Strength of Snow and Straight to the Heart the Snowmen / Grentches have a heightened drop rate.
Participating in Dwayna Vs Grenth will earn 3 for each win.

556.description: Candy Cane Shards are fragments of striped candy cane that drop from slain creatures during the annual Wintersday event. They can be used to make Candy Cane weapons, exchanged for Wintersday Gifts, or used to complete the Frozen Accessory Token quests.

The Jitte is a fairly uncommon type of sword dropped in Factions. Unlike other swords, it deals blunt damage instead of slashing damage.  
Like the Piercing Axe, Colossal Pick, and other weapons that deal nonstandard damage for their weapon types, the Jitte cannot be generated at the PvP creation screen and can only be acquired as a PvE drop or as reward weapon from the Hall of Heroes chest.

37724.acquisition:
Birthday Present: automatically every 12 months from 12 to 84 months.
Birthday Surprise Box: from a Xunlai Gift-Giver in exchange for a Birthday Present Voucher .
37724.description: A Birthday Present is a type of present that is awarded to each character every 12 months (from 12 to 84 months), counting from the day of creation. It appears in inventory the first time that character enters the game after reaching the milestone. A Birthday Present acquired from a Xunlai Gift-Giver is called a Birthday Surprise Box. The main difference between Presents and Surprise Boxes is Surprise Boxes cannot be traded or sold, they will not go into a trade window.
37724.name: Birthday Present

10 are awarded to each member of a winning team in Guild versus Guild during Wintersday. (To prevent exploitation, no rewards are offered if (a) the match ends after 28 minutes without either side killing a Guild Lord or (b) the battle ends in under 5 minutes, e.g. due to one team resigning or zoning .)
Wintersday finale from 2006 onward in Lion's Arch and Kamadan (during and after the rings).

2111.acquisition:
The Tapestry Shred can only be obtained by winning Gwen 's favor in pre-Searing Ascalon. This requires giving her a combination of items: the Broken Flute , a new Flute , a Small Girl's Cape , or Red Iris Flowers ; the last item given must be an iris. The least expensive combination is the broken flute, the replacement flute, and 3 irises. Primary Monks who have completed Monk Test do not need to offer the broken flute.
To get the shred you must initiate conversation with her after giving her these items, but without any of the items in your inventory, such as extra Red Iris Flowers (which you can temporarily drop on the ground first).
2111.description: The Tapestry Shred is a piece of the young Gwen's cape, given to the player as a sign of affection. Years later it can be used in two Tyrian quests.
2111.name: Tapestry Shred

30853.acquisition:
When you talk to M.O.X. for the first time , he will hand a Golem User Manual to you.
If you lose your Golem User Manual, you can acquire a new one from Zinn in his Secret Underground Lair.
If your character has unlocked M.O.X. already and you are in a party with another character that is eligible to acquire him and has not, your party can enter one of the explorable areas where he waits for you for the first time. Talk to him and he will hand you a new copy.
30853.description: The Golem User Manual is a journal written by Zinn about the events that lead up to Zinn's Task.
30853.name: Golem User Manual

28482.description: The Flameseeker Prophecies is a storybook which provides lore for Cooperative Missions completed in Guild Wars Prophecies. There are two separate versions, one for normal mode and one for hard mode; the hard mode version is red and gold rather than red and silver.
28482.name: The Flameseeker Prophecies
